Module Dependency Map

The dependency graph below is extracted from local project imports. Each declaration entry later adds a more focused dependency hint based on the symbols that appear in its source signature and docstring.

QuadraticNumberFields.Basic

This file defines the concept of a quadratic number field as a degree-2 extension of `ℚ`, builds the concrete model `ℚ(√d)` as `QuadraticAlgebra ℚ d 0`, and proves basic properties including norm, trace, and field/number field instances.

Mathlib foundations

QuadraticNumberFields.Euclidean.Basic

Framework for Theorem 10.4 (imaginary quadratic Euclidean classification): for squarefree `d < 0`, the following are equivalent:

QuadraticNumberFields.InstancesQuadraticNumberFields.Basic

QuadraticNumberFields.Examples.ZsqrtdNeg5.Ideals

This file proves the ideal factorization identities and primality results for the non-UFD ring `ℤ[√-5]`, demonstrating the general theory of quadratic number fields on a concrete example.

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals

QuadraticNumberFields.Examples.ZsqrtdNeg5.RamificationInertia

This file computes the ramification indices and inertia degrees for the primes 2 and 3 in `ℤ[√-5]`, demonstrating the fundamental identity `∑ eᵢfᵢ = [K:ℚ]` on a concrete example.

QuadraticNumberFields.Examples.ZsqrtdNeg5.Ideals

QuadraticNumberFields.FieldClassification

This file proves that a field over `ℚ` is quadratic exactly when it is isomorphic to one of the explicit models `Qsqrtd (z : ℚ) = ℚ(√z)` with `z : ℤ` squarefree and `z ≠ 1`.

QuadraticNumberFields.ParametersQuadraticNumberFields.Instances

QuadraticNumberFields.Instances

This file provides `NumberField` instances for quadratic extensions of `ℚ`.

QuadraticNumberFields.Basic

QuadraticNumberFields.Parameters

This file collects the main results about integer parameters for quadratic fields `ℚ(√d)`: rescaling by rational squares, normalization to integer or squarefree integer parameters, and uniqueness of the squarefree parameter.

QuadraticNumberFields.Basic

QuadraticNumberFields.RingEquiv

This file contains **purely categorical** lemmas for transporting algebraic properties across ring equivalences. Nothing here is specific to quadratic fields or number fields — these results work for arbitrary commutative rings.

Mathlib foundations

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Classification

This file proves the classical **ring of integers classification theorem** for quadratic number fields `ℚ(√d)`, where `d` is a squarefree integer with `d ≠ 1`.

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.CommonInstancesQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.IntegralityQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFourQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwoQuadraticNumberFields.RingEquivQuadraticNumberFields.Instances

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.CommonInstances

This file provides global `Fact` instances for frequently-used quadratic field parameters (`d = -1, -3, -5`). These enable cleaner example theorems without verbose inline `letI` blocks.

QuadraticNumberFields.Basic

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Discriminant

This file proves the explicit discriminant formula for `Qsqrtd (d : ℚ)`:

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.CommonInstancesQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Classification

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.HalfInt

This file defines half-integer representatives `(a + b√d) / 2` in quadratic fields and provides basic structural lemmas. These are used in the classification of rings of integers when `d ≡ 1 (mod 4)`.

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Zsqrtd

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Integrality

This file develops integrality criteria for elements of quadratic number fields and proves that integral elements have specific normal forms.

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.HalfIntQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFourQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.TraceNormQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwoQuadraticNumberFields.Parameters

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFour

This file develops congruence criteria modulo 4 used in the classification of rings of integers in quadratic fields. The key distinction is whether `d ≡ 1 (mod 4)`, which determines whether `𝓞(Q(√d))` is `ℤ[√d]` or `ℤ[(1+√d)/2]`.

QuadraticNumberFields.Basic

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Norm

This file formalizes norm properties for quadratic number fields and their rings of integers, with sections separating explicit formulas from the classification-dependent statements.

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Classification

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.TraceNorm

This file isolates the trace/norm computations used in the integrality classification for quadratic number fields.

QuadraticNumberFields.Parameters

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo

This file defines and studies the ring `ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo k`, which is the candidate ring of integers for quadratic fields with `d ≡ 1 (mod 4)`. When `d = 1 + 4k`, we have `𝓞(Q(√d)) ≅ ℤ[(1+√d)/2]`.

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.HalfInt

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Zsqrtd

This module provides a QA-owned model of `ℤ[√d]` based on `QuadraticAlgebra ℤ d 0`, along with its embedding into `Q(√d)` and isomorphism with mathlib's `ℤ√d`.

QuadraticNumberFields.Basic

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals

General results about ideals in the quadratic integer ring `ℤ[√d]`, parameterised by an integer `d` satisfying appropriate arithmetic conditions.

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdMathlibInstances

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdMathlibInstances

This module adds generic algebraic instances for mathlib's `_root_.Zsqrtd d` under useful arithmetic hypotheses.

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ClassificationQuadraticNumberFields.RingEquivQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Zsqrtd

QuadraticNumberFields.TotallyRealComplex

This file classifies quadratic number fields `Q(√d)` according to the sign of `d`:

This section is generated from Lean/QuadraticNumberFields. Each module folds into a declaration table with four views of the same item: the Lean name, the mathematical form it captures, the reason it exists in Lean, and the dependency context it lives in.

QuadraticNumberFields.Basic 11 declarations

This file defines the concept of a quadratic number field as a degree-2 extension of `ℚ`, builds the concrete model `ℚ(√d)` as `QuadraticAlgebra ℚ d 0`, and proves basic properties including norm, trace, and field/number field instances.

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Basic.lean Depends on: Mathlib-only
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
IsQuadraticField
abbrev · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Basic.lean
Lean signature 
abbrev IsQuadraticField (K : Type*) [Field K] [Algebra ℚ K] : Prop :=
 A field `K` is a quadratic field if it is a quadratic extension of `ℚ`. Introduces reusable notation or a concrete carrier so later proofs can stay in the algebraic hierarchy. Foundational
Qsqrtd
abbrev · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Basic.lean
Lean signature 
abbrev Qsqrtd (d : ℚ) : Type := QuadraticAlgebra ℚ d 0
 The quadratic field `ℚ(√d)` as a type alias for `QuadraticAlgebra ℚ d 0`. Introduces reusable notation or a concrete carrier so later proofs can stay in the algebraic hierarchy. Foundational
leftMulMatrix_eq
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Basic.lean
Lean signature 
theorem leftMulMatrix_eq (x : QuadraticAlgebra R a b) : Algebra.leftMulMatrix (basis a b) x = !![x.re, a * x.im; x.im, x.re + b * x.im] := by
 The left multiplication matrix of an element in `QuadraticAlgebra R a b` with respect to the basis `{1, i}`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.Basic` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Foundational
trace_eq_two_re
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Basic.lean
Lean signature 
theorem trace_eq_two_re (x : Qsqrtd d) : Algebra.trace ℚ (Qsqrtd d) x = 2 * x.re := by
 In the model `Q(√d) = QuadraticAlgebra ℚ d 0`, the trace is `2 * re`. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. Qsqrtdtrace
norm
abbrev · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Basic.lean
Lean signature 
abbrev norm {d : ℚ} (x : Qsqrtd d) : ℚ := QuadraticAlgebra.norm x
 The norm of an element `x : Q(√d)`, defined as `N(x) = x · x̄ = x.re² - d · x.im²`. Introduces reusable notation or a concrete carrier so later proofs can stay in the algebraic hierarchy. Qsqrtd
zero_not_isReduced
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Basic.lean
Lean signature 
lemma zero_not_isReduced : ¬ IsReduced (Qsqrtd (0 : ℚ)) := by
 `Q(√0)` is not reduced because `√0² = 0` but `√0 ≠ 0`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.Basic` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Qsqrtd
zero_not_isField
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Basic.lean
Lean signature 
lemma zero_not_isField : ¬ IsField (Qsqrtd (0 : ℚ)) := by
 `Q(√0)` is not a field (it has nilpotents). Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.Basic` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Qsqrtd
one_not_isField
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Basic.lean
Lean signature 
lemma one_not_isField : ¬ IsField (Qsqrtd (1 : ℚ)) := by
 `Q(√1) ≅ ℚ × ℚ` is not a field (it has zero divisors). Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.Basic` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Qsqrtd
eq_one_of_squarefree_isSquare
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Basic.lean
Lean signature 
lemma eq_one_of_squarefree_isSquare {d : ℤ} (hd : Squarefree d) (hsq : IsSquare d) : d = 1 ∨ d = -1 := by
 A squarefree integer that is a perfect square must equal `1` or `-1`. This is a local version until `Squarefree.isUnit_of_isSquare` is merged into mathlib. Foundational
not_isSquare_int_of_squarefree_ne_one
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Basic.lean
Lean signature 
lemma not_isSquare_int_of_squarefree_ne_one {d : ℤ} (hd : Squarefree d) (h1 : d ≠ 1) : ¬ IsSquare d := by
 For a squarefree integer `d ≠ 1`, `d` is not a perfect square in `ℤ`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.Basic` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Foundational
not_isSquare_ratCast_of_squarefree_ne_one
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Basic.lean
Lean signature 
lemma not_isSquare_ratCast_of_squarefree_ne_one {d : ℤ} (hd : Squarefree d) (h1 : d ≠ 1) : ¬ IsSquare ((d : ℤ) : ℚ) := by
 For a squarefree integer `d ≠ 1`, `(d : ℚ)` is not a perfect square in `ℚ`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.Basic` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Foundational

ramificationIdx_P2
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Examples/ZsqrtdNeg5/RamificationInertia.lean
Lean signature 
theorem ramificationIdx_P2 : ramificationIdx (algebraMap ℤ R) (span {(2 : ℤ)}) P2 = 2 := by
 The ramification index of P2 over (2) is 2. Mathematical reason: (2) = P2² in ℤ[√-5], so 2 = e(P2|2) × f(P2|2) = 2 × 1. This means P2 appears with exponent 2 in the factorization of (2). Ideals
ramificationIdx_P3₁
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Examples/ZsqrtdNeg5/RamificationInertia.lean
Lean signature 
theorem ramificationIdx_P3₁ : ramificationIdx (algebraMap ℤ R) (span {(3 : ℤ)}) P3₁ = 1 :=
 The ramification index of P3₁ over (3) is 1. Mathematical reason: (3) = P3₁ · P3₂, so P3₁ appears with exponent 1. Ideals
ramificationIdx_P3₂
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Examples/ZsqrtdNeg5/RamificationInertia.lean
Lean signature 
theorem ramificationIdx_P3₂ : ramificationIdx (algebraMap ℤ R) (span {(3 : ℤ)}) P3₂ = 1 :=
 The ramification index of P3₂ over (3) is 1. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.Examples.ZsqrtdNeg5.RamificationInertia` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Ideals
inertiaDeg_P2
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Examples/ZsqrtdNeg5/RamificationInertia.lean
Lean signature 
theorem inertiaDeg_P2 : inertiaDeg (span {(2 : ℤ)}) P2 = 1 := by
 The inertia degree of P2 over (2) is 1. Mathematical reason: ℤ[√-5]/(2, 1+√-5) ≅ ℤ/2ℤ, so the residue field has size 2, which as a vector space over ℤ/2ℤ has dimension 1. Ideals
inertiaDeg_P3₁
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Examples/ZsqrtdNeg5/RamificationInertia.lean
Lean signature 
theorem inertiaDeg_P3₁ : inertiaDeg (span {(3 : ℤ)}) P3₁ = 1 := by
 The inertia degree of P3₁ over (3) is 1. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.Examples.ZsqrtdNeg5.RamificationInertia` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Ideals

QuadraticNumberFields.FieldClassification 2 declarations

This file proves that a field over `ℚ` is quadratic exactly when it is isomorphic to one of the explicit models `Qsqrtd (z : ℚ) = ℚ(√z)` with `z : ℤ` squarefree and `z ≠ 1`.

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/FieldClassification.lean Depends on: QuadraticNumberFields.ParametersQuadraticNumberFields.Instances
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
exists_squarefree_int_param_of_isQuadraticField
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/FieldClassification.lean
Lean signature 
theorem exists_squarefree_int_param_of_isQuadraticField (K : Type*) [Field K] [Algebra ℚ K] [IsQuadraticField K] :
 Every quadratic field over `ℚ` is isomorphic to `ℚ(√z)` for some squarefree integer parameter `z ≠ 1`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.FieldClassification` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. IsQuadraticField
isQuadraticField_iff_exists_squarefree_int_param
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/FieldClassification.lean
Lean signature 
theorem isQuadraticField_iff_exists_squarefree_int_param (K : Type*) [Field K] [Algebra ℚ K] :
 A field over `ℚ` is quadratic iff it is isomorphic to `ℚ(√z)` for some squarefree integer `z ≠ 1`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.FieldClassification` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. ParametersInstances

QuadraticNumberFields.Parameters 8 declarations

This file collects the main results about integer parameters for quadratic fields `ℚ(√d)`: rescaling by rational squares, normalization to integer or squarefree integer parameters, and uniqueness of the squarefree parameter.

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Parameters.lean Depends on: QuadraticNumberFields.Basic
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
Qsqrtd.rescale
def · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Parameters.lean
Lean signature 
def Qsqrtd.rescale (d : ℚ) (a : ℚ) (ha : a ≠ 0) : Qsqrtd d ≃ₐ[ℚ] Qsqrtd (a ^ 2 * d) := by
 `ℚ(√d) ≃ₐ[ℚ] ℚ(√(a²d))` via `⟨r, s⟩ ↦ ⟨r, s·a⁻¹⟩`. This shows that scaling the parameter by a rational square yields an isomorphic quadratic field. Qsqrtd
Qsqrtd_iso_int_param
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Parameters.lean
Lean signature 
theorem Qsqrtd_iso_int_param (d : ℚ) : ∃ z : ℤ, Nonempty (Qsqrtd d ≃ₐ[ℚ] Qsqrtd (z : ℚ)) := by
 Every quadratic field `Q(√d)` is isomorphic to one with an integer parameter: `∃ z ∈ ℤ, Q(√d) ≃ₐ[ℚ] Q(√z)`. Packages the construction as a map Lean can reuse through `RingHom`, `AlgEquiv`, or rewriting lemmas. Qsqrtd
Qsqrtd_iso_squarefree_int_param
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Parameters.lean
Lean signature 
theorem Qsqrtd_iso_squarefree_int_param {d : ℤ} (hd : d ≠ 0) : ∃ z : ℤ, Squarefree z ∧ Nonempty (Qsqrtd (d : ℚ) ≃ₐ[ℚ] Qsqrtd (z : ℚ)) := by
 Every quadratic field `Q(√d)` is isomorphic to one with a squarefree integer parameter: `∃ z ∈ ℤ, Squarefree z ∧ Q(√d) ≃ₐ[ℚ] Q(√z)`. Packages the construction as a map Lean can reuse through `RingHom`, `AlgEquiv`, or rewriting lemmas. Qsqrtd
not_isSquare_neg_one_rat
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Parameters.lean
Lean signature 
lemma not_isSquare_neg_one_rat : ¬ IsSquare (- (1 : ℚ)) := by
 `-1` is not a square in `ℚ`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.Parameters` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Basic
nat_eq_one_of_squarefree_intcast_of_isSquare
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Parameters.lean
Lean signature 
lemma nat_eq_one_of_squarefree_intcast_of_isSquare (m : ℕ) (hsm : Squarefree (m : ℤ)) (hsq : IsSquare (m : ℤ)) : m = 1 := by
 A squarefree integer that is a perfect square (as an integer) must equal `1`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.Parameters` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Basic
int_dvd_of_ratio_square
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Parameters.lean
Lean signature 
lemma int_dvd_of_ratio_square (d₁ d₂ : ℤ) (hd₂ : d₂ ≠ 0) (hsq_d₂ : Squarefree d₂) (hr : IsSquare ((d₁ : ℚ) / (d₂ : ℚ))) : d₂ ∣ d₁ := by
 If `d₁/d₂` is a rational square and `d₂` is squarefree, then `d₂ ∣ d₁`. This is a **general number-theoretic fact** about squarefree integers: if a squarefree integer divides the denominator of a reduced fraction that is a perfect square, then the denominator must be `1` (since its square part is constrained by squarefreeness). mathlib target: `Mathlib.Data.Int.Squarefree` or `Mathlib.Data.Rat.Squarefree` Basic
squarefree_eq_of_rat_sq_mul
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Parameters.lean
Lean signature 
lemma squarefree_eq_of_rat_sq_mul {d₁ d₂ : ℤ} (hd₁ : Squarefree d₁) (hd₂ : Squarefree d₂) {r : ℚ} (hr : (d₁ : ℚ) = d₂ * r ^ 2) : d₁ = d₂ := by
 If two squarefree integers are related by `d₁ = d₂ · r²` for rational `r`, then `d₁ = d₂`. This is a **key rigidity result for squarefree integers**: two squarefree integers that differ by a rational square factor must be equal. The proof reduces to mutual divisibility via `int_dvd_of_ratio_square`, yielding `d₁ ∣ d₂` and `d₂ ∣ d₁`. The sign ambiguity is resolved by the fact that `-1` is not a rational square. mathlib target: `Mathlib.Data.Int.Squarefree` int_dvd_of_ratio_square
Qsqrtd.param_unique
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/Parameters.lean
Lean signature 
theorem Qsqrtd.param_unique (φ : Qsqrtd (d₁ : ℚ) ≃ₐ[ℚ] Qsqrtd (d₂ : ℚ)) (hsf₁ : Squarefree d₁) (h1₁ : d₁ ≠ 1) (hsf₂ : Squarefree d₂) : d₁ = d₂ := by
 The squarefree integer parameter of a quadratic field is unique: `ℚ(√d₁) ≃ₐ[ℚ] ℚ(√d₂)` with both squarefree and `≠ 1` implies `d₁ = d₂`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.Parameters` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Qsqrtd
QuadraticNumberFields.RingEquiv 3 declarations

This file contains **purely categorical** lemmas for transporting algebraic properties across ring equivalences. Nothing here is specific to quadratic fields or number fields — these results work for arbitrary commutative rings.

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingEquiv.lean Depends on: Mathlib-only
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
dimensionLEOne
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingEquiv.lean
Lean signature 
theorem dimensionLEOne (e : R ≃+* S) [Ring.DimensionLEOne R] : Ring.DimensionLEOne S := by
 Transport `Ring.DimensionLEOne` across a ring equivalence. The proof pulls back prime ideals via `Ideal.comap`, applies `DimensionLEOne` on the source ring, then pushes forward via `Ideal.map`. mathlib target: `Mathlib.RingTheory.Krull` Foundational
isDedekindDomain
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingEquiv.lean
Lean signature 
theorem isDedekindDomain (e : R ≃+* S) [IsDedekindDomain R] : IsDedekindDomain S := by
 Transport `IsDedekindDomain` across a ring equivalence. A Dedekind domain is characterized by: (1) Noetherian, (2) domain, (3) integrally closed, (4) dimension ≤ 1. Each of these is individually invariant under ring isomorphism, so the conjunction is as well. mathlib target: `Mathlib.RingTheory.DedekindDomain.Basic` Foundational
isDedekindDomain_iff
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingEquiv.lean
Lean signature 
theorem isDedekindDomain_iff (e : R ≃+* S) : IsDedekindDomain R ↔ IsDedekindDomain S := by
 `IsDedekindDomain` is invariant under ring equivalence. Turns the classical theorem into a reusable endpoint for later algebraic and arithmetic arguments. Foundational
QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Classification 8 declarations

This file proves the classical **ring of integers classification theorem** for quadratic number fields `ℚ(√d)`, where `d` is a squarefree integer with `d ≠ 1`.

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Classification.lean Depends on: QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.CommonInstancesQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.IntegralityQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFourQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwoQuadraticNumberFields.RingEquivQuadraticNumberFields.Instances
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
ringOfIntegers_equiv_of_integralClosure
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Classification.lean
Lean signature 
theorem ringOfIntegers_equiv_of_integralClosure (K : Type*) [Field K] [NumberField K] (R : Type*) [CommRing R] [Algebra R K] [IsIntegralClosure R ℤ K] :
 Generic fact**: the ring of integers `𝓞 K` is ring-isomorphic to any commutative ring `R` equipped with an `IsIntegralClosure R ℤ K` instance. Packages the construction as a map Lean can reuse through `RingHom`, `AlgEquiv`, or rewriting lemmas. CommonInstancesIntegralityModFour
ringOfIntegers_equiv_of_embedding
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Classification.lean
Lean signature 
theorem ringOfIntegers_equiv_of_embedding (K : Type*) [Field K] [NumberField K] (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (φ : R →+* K) (h_inj : Function.Injective φ) (h_exists : ∀ x : K, IsIntegral ℤ x → ∃ z : R, φ z = x) (h_integral : ∀ z : R, IsIntegral ℤ (φ z)) :
 General criterion for identifying the ring of integers. Packages the construction as a map Lean can reuse through `RingHom`, `AlgEquiv`, or rewriting lemmas. CommonInstancesIntegralityModFour
ringOfIntegers_equiv_zsqrtd_of_mod_four_ne_one
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Classification.lean
Lean signature 
theorem ringOfIntegers_equiv_zsqrtd_of_mod_four_ne_one (hd4 : d % 4 ≠ 1) : Nonempty (𝓞 (Qsqrtd (d : ℚ)) ≃+* Zsqrtd d) := by
 If `d % 4 ≠ 1`, then `𝓞(ℚ(√d)) ≃+* ℤ[√d]`. Packages the construction as a map Lean can reuse through `RingHom`, `AlgEquiv`, or rewriting lemmas. QsqrtdZsqrtd
isDedekindDomain_zsqrtd_of_mod_four_ne_one
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Classification.lean
Lean signature 
theorem isDedekindDomain_zsqrtd_of_mod_four_ne_one (hd4 : d % 4 ≠ 1) : IsDedekindDomain (Zsqrtd d) := by
 If `d % 4 ≠ 1`, then `ℤ[√d]` is a Dedekind domain — it is the full ring of integers of `ℚ(√d)`, and Dedekind-ness transfers from `𝓞(ℚ(√d))` via the ring isomorphism. Turns the classical theorem into a reusable endpoint for later algebraic and arithmetic arguments. Zsqrtd
not_isDedekindDomain_zsqrtd_of_mod_four_eq_one
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Classification.lean
Lean signature 
theorem not_isDedekindDomain_zsqrtd_of_mod_four_eq_one (hd4 : d % 4 = 1) :
 If `d % 4 = 1`, then `ℤ[√d]` is **not** a Dedekind domain. The obstruction is that `ℤ[√d]` is not integrally closed in its fraction field `ℚ(√d)`: the element `ω = (1 + √d)/2` satisfies `ω² − ω − k = 0` (where `d = 1 + 4k`), so it is integral over `ℤ`, but its half-integer coordinates `(1, 1)` are both odd, hence `ω ∉ ℤ[√d]`. Since every Dedekind domain is integrally closed, `ℤ[√d]` cannot be Dedekind. The proof requires `ℚ(√d)` to be the fraction field of `ℤ[√d]`, which is established by a clearing-denominators argument: for `z = r + s√d` with `r = p/q, s = u/v`, the product `qv · z` lies in `ℤ[√d]`. mathlib target: `Mathlib.NumberTheory.Zsqrtd.DedekindDomain`** — the fraction field construction `IsFractionRing (ℤ√d) (ℚ√d)` would also be useful as a standalone result in `Mathlib.NumberTheory.Zsqrtd.Basic`. CommonInstancesIntegralityModFour
isDedekindDomain_zsqrtd_iff_mod_four_ne_one
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Classification.lean
Lean signature 
theorem isDedekindDomain_zsqrtd_iff_mod_four_ne_one : IsDedekindDomain (Zsqrtd d) ↔ d % 4 ≠ 1 := by
 For a squarefree `d ≠ 1`, `ℤ[√d]` is a Dedekind domain if and only if `d ≢ 1 (mod 4)` — equivalently, if and only if `ℤ[√d]` is the full ring of integers of `ℚ(√d)`. This characterizes exactly when the order `ℤ[√d]` coincides with the maximal order `𝓞(ℚ(√d))`. mathlib target: `Mathlib.NumberTheory.Zsqrtd.DedekindDomain` Zsqrtd
ringOfIntegers_equiv_zOnePlusSqrtOverTwo_of_mod_four_eq_one
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Classification.lean
Lean signature 
theorem ringOfIntegers_equiv_zOnePlusSqrtOverTwo_of_mod_four_eq_one (hd4 : d % 4 = 1) :
 If `d % 4 = 1`, writing `d = 1 + 4k`, then `𝓞(ℚ(√d)) ≃+* ℤ[(1+√d)/2]`. The existential `∃ k` witnesses the parametrization; the caller typically already knows `k` from `exists_k_of_mod_four_eq_one`. mathlib target: `Mathlib.NumberTheory.QuadraticField.RingOfIntegers` exists_k_of_mod_four_eq_one
ringOfIntegers_classification
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Classification.lean
Lean signature 
theorem ringOfIntegers_classification :
 Classification of the ring of integers of `ℚ(√d)`. For squarefree `d ≠ 1`, exactly one of the following holds: If `d ≢ 1 (mod 4)`, then `𝓞(ℚ(√d)) ≃+* ℤ[√d]`. If `d ≡ 1 (mod 4)`, then writing `d = 1 + 4k`, `𝓞(ℚ(√d)) ≃+* ℤ[(1+√d)/2]`. This is the classical result found in [Marcus, Theorem 2.16], [Neukirch, I.2], [Stewart–Tall, Theorem 4.6]. mathlib target: `Mathlib.NumberTheory.QuadraticField.RingOfIntegers` CommonInstancesIntegralityModFour
QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.CommonInstances 0 declarations

This file provides global `Fact` instances for frequently-used quadratic field parameters (`d = -1, -3, -5`). These enable cleaner example theorems without verbose inline `letI` blocks.

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Discriminant 13 declarations

This file proves the explicit discriminant formula for `Qsqrtd (d : ℚ)`:

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean Depends on: QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.CommonInstancesQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Classification
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
leftMulMatrix_qa
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean
Lean signature 
theorem leftMulMatrix_qa (a b : ℤ) (x : QuadraticAlgebra ℤ a b) : Algebra.leftMulMatrix (QuadraticAlgebra.basis a b) x = !![x.re, a * x.im; x.im, x.re + b * x.im] := by
 The left-multiplication matrix of `x : QuadraticAlgebra ℤ a b` with respect to the standard basis `{1, ω}` is `[[x.re, a * x.im], [x.im, x.re + b * x.im]]`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Discriminant` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. CommonInstancesClassification
trace_qa
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean
Lean signature 
theorem trace_qa (a b : ℤ) (x : QuadraticAlgebra ℤ a b) : Algebra.trace ℤ (QuadraticAlgebra ℤ a b) x = 2 * x.re + b * x.im := by
 The algebraic trace on `QuadraticAlgebra ℤ a b` is `2 * x.re + b * x.im`. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. trace
traceMatrix_qa
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean
Lean signature 
theorem traceMatrix_qa (a b : ℤ) : Algebra.traceMatrix ℤ (QuadraticAlgebra.basis a b) = !![2, b; b, 2 * a + b ^ 2] := by
 The trace matrix of the standard basis of `QuadraticAlgebra ℤ a b` is `[[2, b], [b, 2 * a + b²]]`. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. trace
discr_qa_basis
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean
Lean signature 
theorem discr_qa_basis (a b : ℤ) : Algebra.discr ℤ (QuadraticAlgebra.basis a b) = 4 * a + b ^ 2 := by
 The discriminant of the standard basis of `QuadraticAlgebra ℤ a b` is `4a + b²`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Discriminant` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. CommonInstancesClassification
discr_zsqrtd_basis
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean
Lean signature 
theorem discr_zsqrtd_basis (d : ℤ) : Algebra.discr ℤ (QuadraticAlgebra.basis d 0 :
 The discriminant of the standard basis of `ℤ[√d]` is `4 * d`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Discriminant` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. CommonInstancesClassification
discr_zOnePlusSqrtOverTwo_basis
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean
Lean signature 
theorem discr_zOnePlusSqrtOverTwo_basis (k : ℤ) : Algebra.discr ℤ (QuadraticAlgebra.basis k 1 :
 The discriminant of the standard basis of `ℤ[(1+√(1+4k))/2]` is `1 + 4 * k`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Discriminant` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. CommonInstancesClassification
ringEquivToIntAlgEquiv
def · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean
Lean signature 
def ringEquivToIntAlgEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [Algebra ℤ R] [CommRing S] [Algebra ℤ S] (e : R ≃+* S) : R ≃ₐ[ℤ] S :=
 Any ring equivalence between `ℤ`-algebras is automatically an `AlgEquiv ℤ`. Introduces reusable notation or a concrete carrier so later proofs can stay in the algebraic hierarchy. CommonInstancesClassification
discr_of_mod_four_ne_one
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean
Lean signature 
theorem discr_of_mod_four_ne_one (hd4 : d % 4 ≠ 1) : NumberField.discr (Qsqrtd (d : ℚ)) = 4 * d := by
 Discriminant of `Q(√d)` when `d % 4 ≠ 1`. When `d % 4 ≠ 1`, the ring of integers is `𝓞 ≅ ℤ[√d]` with ℤ-basis `{1, √d}`, giving discriminant `4d`. Qsqrtd
discr_of_mod_four_eq_one
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean
Lean signature 
theorem discr_of_mod_four_eq_one (hd4 : d % 4 = 1) : NumberField.discr (Qsqrtd (d : ℚ)) = d := by
 Discriminant of `Q(√d)` when `d % 4 = 1`. Uses the ℤ-basis `{1, ω}` where `ω = (1 + √d)/2` for `𝓞 = ℤ[(1+√d)/2]` to compute `disc = d`. Qsqrtd
discr_formula
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean
Lean signature 
theorem discr_formula : NumberField.discr (Qsqrtd (d : ℚ)) = if d % 4 = 1 then d else 4 * d := by
 Unified discriminant formula for `Q(√d)`. For squarefree `d ≠ 1`: `disc(Q(√d)) = d` if `d ≡ 1 (mod 4)` `disc(Q(√d)) = 4d` if `d ≢ 1 (mod 4)` Qsqrtd
discr_gaussian
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean
Lean signature 
theorem discr_gaussian : NumberField.discr (Qsqrtd ((-1 : ℤ) : ℚ)) = -4 :=
 Gaussian integers**: `disc(Q(√(-1))) = -4`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Discriminant` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Qsqrtd
discr_eisenstein
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean
Lean signature 
theorem discr_eisenstein : NumberField.discr (Qsqrtd ((-3 : ℤ) : ℚ)) = -3 :=
 Eisenstein integers**: `disc(Q(√(-3))) = -3`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Discriminant` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Qsqrtd
discr_Qsqrtd_neg_five
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Discriminant.lean
Lean signature 
theorem discr_Qsqrtd_neg_five : NumberField.discr (Qsqrtd ((-5 : ℤ) : ℚ)) = -20 :=
 Q(√(-5))**: `disc(Q(√(-5))) = -20`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Discriminant` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Qsqrtd
QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.HalfInt 4 declarations

This file defines half-integer representatives `(a + b√d) / 2` in quadratic fields and provides basic structural lemmas. These are used in the classification of rings of integers when `d ≡ 1 (mod 4)`.

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/HalfInt.lean Depends on: QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Zsqrtd
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
halfInt
abbrev · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/HalfInt.lean
Lean signature 
abbrev halfInt (d : ℤ) (a' b' : ℤ) : Qsqrtd (d : ℚ) :=
 Canonical half-integer representative `(a' + b'√d)/2` in `Q(√d)`. Introduces reusable notation or a concrete carrier so later proofs can stay in the algebraic hierarchy. Qsqrtd
omegaHalf
abbrev · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/HalfInt.lean
Lean signature 
abbrev omegaHalf (d : ℤ) : Qsqrtd (d : ℚ) := halfInt d 1 1
 The distinguished half-integer generator `(1 + √d)/2`. Introduces reusable notation or a concrete carrier so later proofs can stay in the algebraic hierarchy. QsqrtdhalfInt
trace_halfInt
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/HalfInt.lean
Lean signature 
theorem trace_halfInt (d a' b' : ℤ) : Algebra.trace ℚ (Qsqrtd d) (halfInt d a' b') = a' := by
 Trace of `halfInt` is `a'`. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. QsqrtdhalfInt
norm_halfInt
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/HalfInt.lean
Lean signature 
theorem norm_halfInt (d a' b' : ℤ) : Qsqrtd.norm (halfInt d a' b') = (a' ^ 2 - (d : ℚ) * b' ^ 2) / 4 := by
 Norm of `halfInt` is `(a'² - d*b'²)/4`. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. halfInt
QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Integrality 11 declarations

This file develops integrality criteria for elements of quadratic number fields and proves that integral elements have specific normal forms.

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Integrality.lean Depends on: QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.HalfIntQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFourQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.TraceNormQuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwoQuadraticNumberFields.Parameters
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
dvd_four_sub_sq_iff_exists_zsqrtd_image_of_ne_one_mod_four
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Integrality.lean
Lean signature 
theorem dvd_four_sub_sq_iff_exists_zsqrtd_image_of_ne_one_mod_four (d a' b' : ℤ) (hd : Squarefree d) (hd4 : d % 4 ≠ 1) :
 In the non-`1 mod 4` branch, divisibility by `4` is equivalent to representability in the image of `Zsqrtd d` under the canonical embedding into `Q(√d)`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Integrality` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Zsqrtd
exists_zsqrtd_image_of_dvd_four_sub_sq_of_ne_one_mod_four
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Integrality.lean
Lean signature 
theorem exists_zsqrtd_image_of_dvd_four_sub_sq_of_ne_one_mod_four (d a' b' : ℤ) (hd : Squarefree d) (hd4 : d % 4 ≠ 1) (hdiv : 4 ∣ (a' ^ 2 - d * b' ^ 2)) :

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFour 11 declarations

This file develops congruence criteria modulo 4 used in the classification of rings of integers in quadratic fields. The key distinction is whether `d ≡ 1 (mod 4)`, which determines whether `𝓞(Q(√d))` is `ℤ[√d]` or `ℤ[(1+√d)/2]`.

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ModFour.lean Depends on: QuadraticNumberFields.Basic
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
squarefree_int_not_dvd_four
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ModFour.lean
Lean signature 
lemma squarefree_int_not_dvd_four (d : ℤ) (hd : Squarefree d) : ¬ (4 : ℤ) ∣ d := by
 A squarefree integer is not divisible by `4`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFour` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Basic
squarefree_int_emod_four
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ModFour.lean
Lean signature 
lemma squarefree_int_emod_four (d : ℤ) (hd : Squarefree d) : d % 4 = 1 ∨ d % 4 = 2 ∨ d % 4 = 3 := by
 A squarefree integer has `d % 4 ∈ {1, 2, 3}`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFour` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Basic
Int.sq_emod_four_of_even
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ModFour.lean
Lean signature 
lemma Int.sq_emod_four_of_even (n : ℤ) (h : 2 ∣ n) : n ^ 2 % 4 = 0 := by
 The square of an even integer is `0 mod 4`. This is a pure modular-arithmetic fact: `(2k)² = 4k² ≡ 0 (mod 4)`. mathlib target: `Mathlib.Data.Int.ModCast` or `Mathlib.Data.ZMod.Basic` Basic
Int.sq_emod_four_of_odd
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ModFour.lean
Lean signature 
lemma Int.sq_emod_four_of_odd (n : ℤ) (h : ¬ 2 ∣ n) : n ^ 2 % 4 = 1 := by
 The square of an odd integer is `1 mod 4`. This is a pure modular-arithmetic fact: `(2k+1)² = 4k²+4k+1 ≡ 1 (mod 4)`. mathlib target: `Mathlib.Data.Int.ModCast` or `Mathlib.Data.ZMod.Basic` Basic
dvd_four_sub_sq_iff_even_even_or_odd_odd_mod_four_one
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ModFour.lean
Lean signature 
theorem dvd_four_sub_sq_iff_even_even_or_odd_odd_mod_four_one (d a' b' : ℤ) (hd : Squarefree d) : 4 ∣ (a' ^ 2 - d * b' ^ 2) ↔ (2 ∣ a' ∧ 2 ∣ b') ∨ (¬ 2 ∣ a' ∧ ¬ 2 ∣ b' ∧ d % 4 = 1) := by
 Main mod-4 criterion for the binary quadratic form `a'² − d·b'²`. `4 ∣ (a'² − d·b'²)` if and only if either both `a', b'` are even, or both are odd and `d ≡ 1 (mod 4)`. This is the key arithmetic fact driving the classification of rings of integers: it determines whether the half-integer `(a' + b'√d)/2` can be integral. mathlib target: `Mathlib.NumberTheory.QuadraticField.RingOfIntegers` Basic
even_even_of_dvd_four_sub_sq_of_ne_one_mod_four
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ModFour.lean
Lean signature 
theorem even_even_of_dvd_four_sub_sq_of_ne_one_mod_four (d a' b' : ℤ) (hd : Squarefree d) (hd4 : d % 4 ≠ 1) (h : 4 ∣ (a' ^ 2 - d * b' ^ 2)) :
 If `d % 4 ≠ 1`, divisibility by `4` forces both numerators even. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFour` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Basic
dvd_four_sub_sq_iff_even_even_of_ne_one_mod_four
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ModFour.lean
Lean signature 
theorem dvd_four_sub_sq_iff_even_even_of_ne_one_mod_four (d a' b' : ℤ) (hd : Squarefree d) (hd4 : d % 4 ≠ 1) :
 If `d % 4 ≠ 1`, divisibility by `4` is equivalent to both numerators even. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFour` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Basic
dvd_four_sub_sq_iff_same_parity_of_one_mod_four
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ModFour.lean
Lean signature 
theorem dvd_four_sub_sq_iff_same_parity_of_one_mod_four (d a' b' : ℤ) (hd : Squarefree d) (hd4 : d % 4 = 1) :
 If `d % 4 = 1`, divisibility by `4` is equivalent to same parity. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFour` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Basic

theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ModFour.lean
Lean signature 
theorem exists_k_of_mod_four_eq_one {d : ℤ} (hd4 : d % 4 = 1) : ∃ k : ℤ, d = 1 + 4 * k := by
 Extract parameterization `d = 1 + 4k` from `d % 4 = 1`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFour` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Basic

 The converse of `exists_k_of_mod_four_eq_one`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFour` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. exists_k_of_mod_four_eq_one

Lean signature

 Canonical branch split by `d % 4 = 1`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ModFour` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Basic
QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Norm 14 declarations

This file formalizes norm properties for quadratic number fields and their rings of integers, with sections separating explicit formulas from the classification-dependent statements.

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Norm.lean Depends on: QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Classification
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
norm_mul
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Norm.lean
Lean signature 
theorem norm_mul (d : ℚ) (x y : Qsqrtd d) : Qsqrtd.norm (x * y) = Qsqrtd.norm x * Qsqrtd.norm y :=
 The norm on `Q(√d)` is multiplicative: `N(xy) = N(x) N(y)`. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. Qsqrtdnorm
norm_one
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Norm.lean
Lean signature 
theorem norm_one (d : ℚ) : Qsqrtd.norm (1 : Qsqrtd d) = 1 :=
 The norm maps `1` to `1`. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. Qsqrtdnorm
norm_zsqrtd
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Norm.lean
Lean signature 
theorem norm_zsqrtd (d : ℤ) (z : Zsqrtd d) : Zsqrtd.norm z = z.re ^ 2 - d * z.im ^ 2 := by
 The norm of an element of `Zsqrtd d` is an integer: `N(a + b√d) = a² - d·b²`. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. Zsqrtdnorm
norm_mul_zsqrtd
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Norm.lean
Lean signature 
theorem norm_mul_zsqrtd (d : ℤ) (x y : Zsqrtd d) : Zsqrtd.norm (x * y) = Zsqrtd.norm x * Zsqrtd.norm y :=
 The norm on `Zsqrtd d` is multiplicative. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. Zsqrtdnorm
norm_zsqrtd_toQsqrtd
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Norm.lean
Lean signature 
theorem norm_zsqrtd_toQsqrtd (d : ℤ) (z : Zsqrtd d) : Qsqrtd.norm (Zsqrtd.toQsqrtd z) = (Zsqrtd.norm z : ℚ) := by
 The norm of `a + b√d` embeds to `a² - d·b²` in `ℚ`. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. Zsqrtdnorm
norm_mem_zsqrtd
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Norm.lean
Lean signature 
theorem norm_mem_zsqrtd (d : ℤ) (z : Zsqrtd d) : ∃ n : ℤ, Qsqrtd.norm (Zsqrtd.toQsqrtd z) = n := by
 For `d % 4 ≠ 1`, elements of `ℤ[√d]` have integer norm after embedding. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. Zsqrtdnorm
norm_mul_zOnePlusSqrtOverTwo
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Norm.lean
Lean signature 
theorem norm_mul_zOnePlusSqrtOverTwo (k : ℤ) (x y : ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo k) : QuadraticAlgebra.norm (x * y) = QuadraticAlgebra.norm x * QuadraticAlgebra.norm y :=
 The norm on `ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo k` is multiplicative. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwonorm
norm_zOnePlusSqrtOverTwo
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Norm.lean
Lean signature 
theorem norm_zOnePlusSqrtOverTwo (k : ℤ) (z : ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo k) : QuadraticAlgebra.norm z = z.re ^ 2 + z.re * z.im - k * z.im ^ 2 := by
 The norm of an element of `ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo k` is `a² + a·b - k·b²`. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwonorm

theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Norm.lean

 The norm of `a + b·ω` embeds correctly to `ℚ`. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwonorm
norm_mem_zOnePlusSqrtOverTwo
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Norm.lean
Lean signature 
theorem norm_mem_zOnePlusSqrtOverTwo (k : ℤ) (z : ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo k) : ∃ n : ℤ, Qsqrtd.norm (ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo.toQsqrtdHom k z) = n := by
 For `d % 4 = 1`, elements of `ℤ[(1+√d)/2]` have integer norm after embedding. Bridges the textbook invariant with a form that is convenient for downstream automation and rewriting. ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwonorm
norm_mem_ringOfIntegers
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Norm.lean
Lean signature 
theorem norm_mem_ringOfIntegers (α : 𝓞 (Qsqrtd (d : ℚ))) : ∃ n : ℤ, Qsqrtd.norm (α : Qsqrtd (d : ℚ)) = n := by
 For `α ∈ 𝓞(Q(√d))`, the norm `N(α)` is an integer. This follows from the classification theorem: elements of the ring of integers live in either `ℤ[√d]` or `ℤ[(1+√d)/2]`, both of which have integer-valued norm. TODO: This proof requires establishing that the ring isomorphism from the classification commutes with the coercion to the number field. Qsqrtdnorm

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo 12 declarations

This file defines and studies the ring `ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo k`, which is the candidate ring of integers for quadratic fields with `d ≡ 1 (mod 4)`. When `d = 1 + 4k`, we have `𝓞(Q(√d)) ≅ ℤ[(1+√d)/2]`.

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo.lean Depends on: QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.HalfInt
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
d_of_k
abbrev · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo.lean
Lean signature 
abbrev d_of_k (k : ℤ) : ℚ := ((1 + 4 * k : ℤ) : ℚ)
 The discriminant-like parameter `1 + 4k` viewed in `ℚ`. Introduces reusable notation or a concrete carrier so later proofs can stay in the algebraic hierarchy. HalfInt
omega
def · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo.lean
Lean signature 
def omega (k : ℤ) : Qsqrtd (d_of_k k) := ⟨(1 / 2 : ℚ), (1 / 2 : ℚ)⟩
 `ω_k = (1 + √(1 + 4k)) / 2` in `Q(√(1 + 4k))`. Introduces reusable notation or a concrete carrier so later proofs can stay in the algebraic hierarchy. Qsqrtdd_of_k
Zomega
abbrev · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo.lean
Lean signature 
abbrev Zomega (k : ℤ) : Subalgebra ℤ (Qsqrtd (d_of_k k)) :=
 The order candidate `ℤ[ω_k]` with `ω_k = (1 + √(1 + 4k)) / 2`. Introduces reusable notation or a concrete carrier so later proofs can stay in the algebraic hierarchy. Qsqrtdd_of_k
omega_mem_Zomega
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo.lean
Lean signature 
lemma omega_mem_Zomega (k : ℤ) : omega k ∈ Zomega k :=
 A proved statement exported by `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. omegaZomega
ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo
abbrev · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo.lean
Lean signature 
abbrev ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo (d : ℤ) : Type := QuadraticAlgebra ℤ d 1
 Algebraic model of `ℤ[(1 + √(1 + 4d))/2]` via `ω^2 = ω + d`. In `QuadraticAlgebra R a b`, one has `ω^2 = a + b * ω`, so this is `QuadraticAlgebra ℤ d 1` (not `QuadraticAlgebra ℤ 1 d`). HalfInt
qParam
abbrev · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo.lean
Lean signature 
abbrev qParam (d : ℤ) : ℚ := Qsqrtd.d_of_k d
 Ambient parameter in `ℚ`: `1 + 4d`. Introduces reusable notation or a concrete carrier so later proofs can stay in the algebraic hierarchy. HalfInt
toQsqrtdFun
def · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo.lean
Lean signature 
def toQsqrtdFun (d : ℤ) : ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo d → Qsqrtd (qParam d) :=

carrierSet
def · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo.lean
Lean signature 
def carrierSet (d : ℤ) : Set (Qsqrtd (qParam d)) := Set.range (toQsqrtdFun d)
 Candidate carrier set of `ℤ[(1 + √(1 + 4d))/2]` inside `Q(√(1 + 4d))`. Introduces reusable notation or a concrete carrier so later proofs can stay in the algebraic hierarchy. QsqrtdqParamtoQsqrtdFun
halfInt_mem_carrierSet_iff_same_parity
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo.lean
Lean signature 
theorem halfInt_mem_carrierSet_iff_same_parity (k a' b' : ℤ) : (∃ z : ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo k, toQsqrtdFun k z = QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.halfInt (1 + 4 * k) a' b') ↔ a' % 2 = b' % 2 := by
 A half-integer element belongs to the `ℤ[(1 + √(1+4k))/2]` carrier iff the two numerators have the same parity. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwotoQsqrtdFun

 Equivalent set-membership form of `halfInt_mem_carrierSet_iff_same_parity`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZOnePlusSqrtOverTwo` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. carrierSethalfInt_mem_carrierSet_iff_same_parity
QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.Zsqrtd 17 declarations

This module provides a QA-owned model of `ℤ[√d]` based on `QuadraticAlgebra ℤ d 0`, along with its embedding into `Q(√d)` and isomorphism with mathlib's `ℤ√d`.

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Zsqrtd.lean Depends on: QuadraticNumberFields.Basic
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
Zsqrtd
abbrev · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Zsqrtd.lean
Lean signature 
abbrev Zsqrtd (d : ℤ) : Type := QuadraticAlgebra ℤ d 0
 QA base model of `ℤ[√d]` reusing `QuadraticAlgebra`. Introduces reusable notation or a concrete carrier so later proofs can stay in the algebraic hierarchy. Basic

sqrtd
abbrev · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Zsqrtd.lean

abbrev sqrtd : Zsqrtd d := ⟨0, 1⟩

abbrev · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Zsqrtd.lean

halfInt_mem_range_toQsqrtdHom_iff_even_even
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/Zsqrtd.lean

theorem halfInt_mem_range_toQsqrtdHom_iff_even_even (d a' b' : ℤ) : (∃ z : Zsqrtd d, toQsqrtdHom d z = halfInt (d := d) a' b') ↔ (2 ∣ a' ∧ 2 ∣ b') := by

QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals 15 declarations

General results about ideals in the quadratic integer ring `ℤ[√d]`, parameterised by an integer `d` satisfying appropriate arithmetic conditions.

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZsqrtdIdeals.lean Depends on: QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdMathlibInstances
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
span_le_span_singleton_of_forall_dvd
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZsqrtdIdeals.lean
Lean signature 
theorem span_le_span_singleton_of_forall_dvd {α : Type*} [CommSemiring α] {a : α} {S : Set α} (h : ∀ x ∈ S, a ∣ x) :
 If `a` divides every element of `S`, then `Ideal.span S ≤ Ideal.span {a}`. This is a **general ideal-lattice lemma** for any commutative semiring — it has nothing to do with `ℤ[√d]` and would be useful throughout ring theory. mathlib target: `Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Operations` ZsqrtdMathlibInstances
ideal_of_prime_norm_is_prime
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZsqrtdIdeals.lean
Lean signature 
theorem ideal_of_prime_norm_is_prime {R : Type*} [CommRing R] [IsDedekindDomain R] [Module.Free ℤ R] (I : Ideal R) (hI : I.absNorm.Prime) : I.IsPrime :=
 An ideal whose absolute norm is a prime number is a prime ideal. This is a **general fact for Dedekind domains** — an ideal with prime norm is irreducible in the ideal monoid, hence prime by unique factorization of ideals. mathlib target: `Mathlib.RingTheory.DedekindDomain.Ideal` norm
map_span_int_singleton
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZsqrtdIdeals.lean
Lean signature 
lemma map_span_int_singleton (n : ℤ) : Ideal.map (algebraMap ℤ (Zsqrtd d)) (Ideal.span {n}) = Ideal.span {(n : Zsqrtd d)} := by
 A proved statement exported by `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Zsqrtd
mul_re_add_im_eq
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZsqrtdIdeals.lean
Lean signature 
lemma mul_re_add_im_eq (a b : Zsqrtd d) : (a * b).re + (a * b).im = (a.re + a.im) * (b.re + b.im) + (d - 1) * a.im * b.im := by
 The fundamental identity for `re + im` of a product in `ℤ[√d]`: `(a*b).re + (a*b).im = (a.re + a.im) * (b.re + b.im) + (d - 1) * a.im * b.im`. This identity is purely algebraic in the multiplication law of `QuadraticAlgebra ℤ d 0`. It shows that the "augmentation" map `z ↦ z.re + z.im` is *almost* multiplicative, with a correction term proportional to `d - 1`. When `p ∣ (d - 1)`, the correction vanishes mod `p`, making the augmentation a ring hom `ℤ[√d] → ℤ/pℤ`. mathlib target: `Mathlib.NumberTheory.Zsqrtd.Basic` Zsqrtd
mul_re_sub_im_eq
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZsqrtdIdeals.lean
Lean signature 
lemma mul_re_sub_im_eq (a b : Zsqrtd d) : (a * b).re - (a * b).im = (a.re - a.im) * (b.re - b.im) + (d - 1) * a.im * b.im := by
 The fundamental identity for `re - im` of a product in `ℤ[√d]`: `(a*b).re - (a*b).im = (a.re - a.im) * (b.re - b.im) + (d - 1) * a.im * b.im`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Zsqrtd
liftModP_apply
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZsqrtdIdeals.lean
Lean signature 
lemma liftModP_apply (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] (hd : (p : ℤ) ∣ (d - 1)) (z : Zsqrtd d) : liftModP p hd z = (z.re + z.im : ZMod p) := by
 A proved statement exported by `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Zsqrtd
liftModPNeg_apply
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZsqrtdIdeals.lean
Lean signature 
lemma liftModPNeg_apply (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] (hd : (p : ℤ) ∣ (d - 1)) (z : Zsqrtd d) : liftModPNeg p hd z = (z.re - z.im : ZMod p) := by
 A proved statement exported by `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Zsqrtd
mem_span_p_one_minus_sqrtd_iff
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZsqrtdIdeals.lean
Lean signature 
lemma mem_span_p_one_minus_sqrtd_iff (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] (hd : (p : ℤ) ∣ (d - 1)) (z : Zsqrtd d) :
 An element of `ℤ[√d]` belongs to `(p, 1-√d)` iff `p ∣ (re + im)`, provided `p ∣ (d - 1)` for a prime `p`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Zsqrtd
mem_span_p_one_plus_sqrtd_iff
lemma · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZsqrtdIdeals.lean
Lean signature 
lemma mem_span_p_one_plus_sqrtd_iff (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] (hd : (p : ℤ) ∣ (d - 1)) (z : Zsqrtd d) :
 An element of `ℤ[√d]` belongs to `(p, 1+√d)` iff `p ∣ (re - im)`, provided `p ∣ (d - 1)` for a prime `p`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Zsqrtd
isPrime_span_p_one_minus_sqrtd
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZsqrtdIdeals.lean
Lean signature 
theorem isPrime_span_p_one_minus_sqrtd (p : ℕ) [Fact p.Prime] (hd : (p : ℤ) ∣ (d - 1)) : IsPrime (Ideal.span ({(p : Zsqrtd d), 1 - sqrtd} : Set (Zsqrtd d)) : Ideal (Zsqrtd d)) := by
 The ideal `(p, 1-√d)` is prime in `ℤ[√d]` when `p ∣ (d - 1)` for a prime `p`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Zsqrtdsqrtd

 The ideal `(p, 1+√d)` is prime in `ℤ[√d]` when `p ∣ (d - 1)` for a prime `p`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.RingOfIntegers.ZsqrtdIdeals` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Zsqrtdsqrtd

isDedekindDomain_iff_mod_four_ne_one
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/RingOfIntegers/ZsqrtdMathlibInstances.lean
Lean signature 
theorem isDedekindDomain_iff_mod_four_ne_one :
 For a squarefree `d ≠ 1`, mathlib's `ℤ√d` is Dedekind exactly in the `d % 4 ≠ 1` branch. Turns the classical theorem into a reusable endpoint for later algebraic and arithmetic arguments. ClassificationRingEquivZsqrtd
QuadraticNumberFields.TotallyRealComplex 4 declarations

This file classifies quadratic number fields `Q(√d)` according to the sign of `d`:

Source: Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/TotallyRealComplex.lean Depends on: QuadraticNumberFields.InstancesQuadraticNumberFields.Basic
Declaration Mathematical Form Lean Motivation Dependency Focus
embedding_omega_sq
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/TotallyRealComplex.lean
Lean signature 
theorem embedding_omega_sq (v : NumberField.InfinitePlace (Qsqrtd (d : ℚ))) :
 For any infinite place `v` of `Q(√d)`, the image of `ω` satisfies `φ(ω)² = d`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.TotallyRealComplex` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Qsqrtd
isTotallyReal
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/TotallyRealComplex.lean
Lean signature 
theorem isTotallyReal (hd : 0 < d) : NumberField.IsTotallyReal (Qsqrtd (d : ℚ)) := by
 A real quadratic field `Q(√d)` with `d > 0` is totally real: all embeddings into `ℂ` have image contained in `ℝ`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.TotallyRealComplex` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Qsqrtd
isTotallyComplex
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/TotallyRealComplex.lean
Lean signature 
theorem isTotallyComplex (hd : d < 0) : NumberField.IsTotallyComplex (Qsqrtd (d : ℚ)) := by
 An imaginary quadratic field `Q(√d)` with `d < 0` is totally complex: no embedding into `ℂ` has image contained in `ℝ`. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.TotallyRealComplex` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Qsqrtd
isCMField
theorem · Lean/QuadraticNumberFields/TotallyRealComplex.lean
Lean signature 
theorem isCMField (hd : d < 0) : NumberField.IsCMField (Qsqrtd (d : ℚ)) := by
 An imaginary quadratic field `Q(√d)` with `d < 0` is a CM field. Provides a named lemma in `QuadraticNumberFields.TotallyRealComplex` so later files can depend on it without reproving the argument. Qsqrtd
